channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Improve Filament Import UX with Persistent Error CSV Downloads
item.description: By default, FilamentPHP provides a convenient import action with built-in feedback once the process is complete. After an import finishes, a notification is displayed showing the result, along...

item.title: Moving 1,100 Paying Customers Between Stripe Accounts in a 3-Hour Window
item.description: In a single 3-hour window at midnight US time, we moved 2,500 US customers from our Australian Stripe account to a new US Stripe account. Payment methods, credits, coupons, promo codes, and...

item.title: The first time you watch an AI agent buy something, you will feel something you cannot name.
item.description: A 91 second experiment, an $11.78 charge, and a moment of hesitation that surprised me more than the result. I knew the agent was going to spend money. I had set the cap. I had created the Stripe...

item.title: Beyond Localhost: Implementing Production-Grade Entra ID Auth in .NET Aspire
item.description: We’ve all been there. You spend two days wiring up authentication for a distributed project. It works perfectly on your machine with a mock provider or a local database, but the moment you try to...

item.title: FastMCP: Build Production-Ready MCP Servers in Python with Minimal Boilerplate
item.description: Hello, I'm Shrijith Venkatramana. I'm building git-lrc, an AI code reviewer that runs on every commit. Star Us to help devs discover the project. Do give it a try and share your feedback for...

item.title: Ashnymph’s Childhood EP is exhilarating dance goth rock
item.description: I've got to thank my oldest friend and concert buddy, Tim, for turning me on to this one. Ashnymph is a London band that blends post-punk melodies with Krautrock rhythms and industrial grime....

item.title: Getting Started with Python For Data Analytics: A Beginner-Friendly Introduction
item.description: What is Python? Python is a beginner-friendly, "human-readable" programming language that acts like a live translator, allowing you to quickly connect different data tools and organize your work...

item.title: Building an AI That Reads Tarot Cards (and Actually Remembers Your Last Reading)
item.description: Most tarot apps work like this: shuffle, deal, look up card meaning from a table, display. It's a random number generator wearing a mystical costume. I wanted to build something that actually...

item.title: Debugging confidently wrong answers from LLM-powered features
item.description: The bug that took two weeks to surface A few months back I shipped a feature that used a language model to summarize support tickets and suggest responses. Internal QA loved it. The demo went...

item.title: Terminal themes optimize for code. Claude Code isn't code.
item.description: Most of what Claude Code outputs is English. Tool call results, reasoning traces, permission prompts, multi-paragraph explanations — on a busy session my screen is maybe 20% syntax-highlighted...

item.title: I shipped 30 apps. AI crawlers ignore them.
item.description: I have shipped over 30 apps. Most of them are absolute ghosts right now. If you ask an AI tool to find something that does exactly what my projects do, they won't show up. It is not because the...

item.title: We’re feeling cynical about xAI’s big deal with Anthropic
item.description: On the latest episode of the Equity podcast, we discussed what xAI's deal with Anthropic might mean for parent company SpaceX.

item.title: Cricut’s $99 craft cutting machine helped me feel creative again
item.description: I've always been skeptical of products that claim to help you lead a more creative life. But one recently won me over. I've spent three weeks with the Cricut Joy 2, a smart cutting and drawing...

item.title: Writers are fleeing the Substack Tax
item.description: Substack, the once buzzy newsletter platform, is losing a new swath of writers to rival platforms most people haven't heard of. Just last month, The Ankler, one of Substack's most popular...

item.title: Ollama Out-of-Bounds Read Vulnerability Allows Remote Process Memory Leak
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a critical security vulnerability in Ollama that, if successfully exploited, could allow a remote, unauthenticated attacker to leak its entire process...

item.title: Netflix may have finally figured out games
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on Netflix, follow Andrew Webster. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes at 8AM...

item.title: Vivo’s X300 Ultra has the best cameras in any phone
item.description: A few months ago, I wrote that the telephoto camera is the only lens that matters any more, at least when it comes to Ultra-class flagships. As phones got better, cameras became where...

item.title: Voice AI in India is hard. Wispr Flow is betting on it anyway.
item.description: Wispr Flow says growth accelerated in India after its Hinglish rollout, even as voice AI products continue to face challenges.

item.title: So you’ve heard these AI terms and nodded along; let’s fix that
item.description: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

item.title: "OncoAgent: A Dual-Tier Multi-Agent Framework for Privacy-Preserving Oncology Clinical Decision Support"
Dyson’s powerful 360 Vis Nav robovac is down to $279.99 for a limited time
item.description: If you’re tired of running your vacuum multiple times just to get the dirt and debris out of the carpets in your living room, Dyson’s 360 Vis Nav is worth a look. It’s one of the more powerful...

item.title: Nvidia has already committed $40B to equity AI deals this year
item.description: Nvidia continues to be a big investor in the AI ecosystem.

item.title: These great digital gifts will arrive just in time for Mother’s Day
item.description: And just like that, Mother’s Day is tomorrow, May 10th, which is too soon for most online purchases to arrive in time. That said, you aren’t alone if you waited too long to pick up a gift this...

item.title: Let it snow
item.description: Water gets all the credit. When gaming companies want to show off new graphics technology, things tend to get wet; splashing waves that are only possible with the latest physics engine, or puddles...

item.title: Quantum dot TVs beat RGB LED TVs, says the company that makes QDs for TVs

item.title: Hackable Robot Lawn Mower Unlocks a New Nightmare
item.description: Plus: Meta officially kills encrypted Instagram DMs, the Trump administration targets “violent left wing extremists,” leaked documents reveal Russia's school for elite hackers, and more.

item.title: cPanel, WHM Release Fixes for Three New Vulnerabilities — Patch Now
item.description: cPanel has released updates to address three vulnerabilities in cPanel and Web Host Manager (WHM) that could be exploited to achieve privilege escalation, code execution, and denial-of-service....

item.title: Musk v. Altman week 2: OpenAI fires back, and Shivon Zilis reveals that Musk tried to poach Sam Altman
item.description: In the second week of the landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI, Musk’s motivations for bringing the suit were under scrutiny. Last week, Musk took the stand, alleging that OpenAI CEO Sam...

item.title: Laid-off Oracle workers tried to negotiate better severance. Oracle said no.
item.description: Some found out they didn't qualify for WARN Act protections like two-months notice because the company had classified them as remote workers.
